main_paper_allowed_claims: STWM predicts future semantic trace-unit fields over frozen video-derived trace/semantic states.
STWM improves changed semantic prototype prediction over copy and strong controlled copy-aware baselines while preserving stable semantic memory.
Future rollout hidden is load-bearing at H8 and remains load-bearing at H16/H24 under the frozen-cache protocol.
C32 is the best prototype-vocabulary tradeoff under val-only selection.
Trace-belief / TUSB utility provides supporting future identity association evidence.
External SAM2/CoTracker/Cutie comparisons define the boundary of STWM rather than the core same-output claim.
ostf_allowed_claims: Real CoTracker-official object-dense teacher caches are feasible and validated.
OSTF V17-V22 provides an honest diagnostic progression and appendix story.
V21 oracle best-of-K / minFDE / MissRate diagnostics show multimodal dense futures can contain useful alternatives on hard subsets.
Current CoTracker-teacher H8/H16 setup remains CV-dominated at deployable top-1 mode selection.
ostf_forbidden_claims: Object-dense semantic trace field world model is established in the current setup.
OSTF beats constant velocity in the current setup.
Dense field should become a main-paper claim now.
V21 oracle best-of-K should be presented as a deployable top-1 result.
non_blocking_live_repo_caveats: gitignored assets/artifacts mean exported snapshots can omit visualization packs and OSTF caches even when the live repo is complete
external boundary rerun payload is not packaged as a clean raw-frame benchmark bundle in the current export
